senses.definition: to leave a place by driving a vehicle

examples.x: As the sun set, they decided to drive away from the beach and head home.
After the event, the attendees slowly began to drive away from the venue.
The family gathered their belongings and prepared to drive away after the weekend getaway.
The taxi driver waited for the passengers to finish loading their luggage before they could drive away.
The group of friends made plans to drive away for a spontaneous road trip.

senses.definition: to start driving away from a location

examples.x: He waved goodbye and started to drive off towards the highway.
She forgot her sunglasses and had to drive off again after stopping.
They decided to drive off early to avoid the traffic jam.
I always double-check my mirrors before I drive off.
The car hesitated for a moment before smoothly driving off to the road.

senses.definition: to continue driving a vehicle

examples.x: The cars are driving on despite the heavy rain.
He drove on even though he was feeling tired.
She has driven on this road many times before.
We will drive on until we find a place to rest.
They drive on the highway every morning to get to work.

senses.definition: to cause a machine or device to begin operating or functioning

examples.x: She started the coffee machine to brew a fresh pot for the morning.
The technician started the computer to perform diagnostic tests.
He pressed the button to start the washing machine and began his laundry.
To get the engine running, he turned the key and started the car.
The operator started the assembly line to begin production for the day.
The pilot started the aircraft engines as they prepared for takeoff.
He had to start the generator manually during the power outage.

senses.definition: to make a vehicle, machine or object move more quickly

examples.x: The pilot smoothly pushed the throttle forward to accelerate the airplane for takeoff.
The engineer fine-tuned the engine to accelerate the conveyor belt.
The driver accelerated the car to overtake the slow-moving vehicle ahead.
The captain ordered the helmsman to accelerate the ship to escape an approaching storm.
The pilot skillfully accelerated the jet to quickly climb to a higher altitude.

senses.definition: to slow down or stop a moving car, etc. by using the brakes

examples.x: The cyclist had to quickly brake to avoid colliding with the pedestrian crossing the street.
As the train approached the station, the engineer began to gradually brake to ensure a gentle and precise stop.
The skilled skater knew exactly when to brake, gracefully slowing down.
The instructor taught the new driver how to properly brake in various situations.
In heavy traffic, it's essential to maintain a safe following distance and be prepared to brake quickly if needed.

senses.definition: to press the accelerator pedal down as far as it will go to make the vehicle go very fast
to control the direction of a moving object, such as a car, ship, etc.

examples.x: He expertly steered the car through the winding mountain roads.
The captain skillfully steered the ship through the narrow channel.
In adverse weather conditions, the captain faced the challenge of steering the sailboat safely back to harbor.
The young driver learned to steer the car smoothly.
She steered the plane smoothly onto the runway for landing.

senses.definition: to change to a higher gear in a vehicle to go faster

examples.x: The driver decided to upshift as he entered the highway.
You need to upshift when the engine starts to sound too loud.
She forgot to upshift, and the car was moving very slowly.
The instructor reminded the student to upshift while accelerating.
It is important to upshift smoothly to avoid jerking the car.

senses.definition: a shift to a lower gear in a vehicle's transmission

examples.x: He performed a downshift to tackle the steep hill.
She heard the engine rev higher after the downshift.
The downshift helped maintain control on the slippery road.
Make sure to perform downshift early enough to avoid engine strain.

senses.definition: to change to a lower gear in a vehicle to reduce speed or increase power

examples.x: The driver gears down as he approaches the sharp turn.
She will need to gear down when going up the steep hill.
The instructor explained why it's important to gear down before stopping.
They have geared down to control the speed on the icy road.
We were told to gear down to save fuel in heavy traffic.

senses.definition: to cause or maneuver a vehicle to move backward
to decrease the speed of something

examples.x: The driver slowed the car as they approached the intersection.
She slowed the treadmill to a walking pace after an intense workout.
The cyclist slowed his bike to let the pedestrians cross the road.
The pilot slowed the aircraft during the descent for a smooth landing.
The technician slowed the conveyor belt to avoid jamming the production line.

senses.definition: to travel at a consistent and efficient speed

examples.x: The pilot instructed the airplane crew to cruise at a specific altitude and speed.
The ship captain directed the crew to cruise at a moderate speed once they reached open waters.
To conserve energy, the driver decided to cruise on the highway.
The cyclist adjusted the bike's gears to cruise on the flat terrain
The eco-conscious commuter preferred to cruise at a moderate speed on their scooter.

senses.definition: to change the direction of something's movement by rotating or steering it
to go around or encircle an object or obstacle, allowing movement to continue in a changed direction

examples.x: In the parking lot, the driver had to round the construction barriers to access the open road.
The hikers decided to round the large boulder obstructing the trail to reach the summit.
To avoid the traffic jam, the savvy driver chose to round the city center through less congested streets.
The cyclist skillfully rounded the sharp curve in the road, adjusting speed for a smooth transition.
The runners had to round the fallen tree on the trail and continue the race.

senses.definition: to abruptly turn to a different direction

examples.x: The driver had to veer sharply to the right to avoid colliding with the stalled car.
To avoid a pedestrian stepping into the bike lane, the cyclist had to veer to the left.
The hiker had to veer off the trail when they encountered a fallen tree blocking their path.
The raft veered suddenly as it navigated through the rapids.
Realizing another skier was on a collision course, she had to veer to the side to avoid an accident on the slopes.

senses.definition: to alter the direction of a vehicle or object

examples.x: The car turned off sharply to avoid colliding with the oncoming traffic.
The road turned off unexpectedly, leading us to a hidden scenic spot.
He turned off the sailboat gently, catching the wind from a new angle.
The pilot turned off the aircraft smoothly towards the landing strip.

senses.definition: to change the originally planned path or direction of something, especially in transportation

examples.x: Due to the road closure, the traffic management system had to reroute vehicles through alternative streets.
The airline had to reroute the flight to avoid severe weather conditions, ensuring passenger safety.
The GPS app automatically rerouted the driver when it detected heavy congestion on the original route.
In response to a security threat, authorities decided to reroute the parade away from the crowded city center.
The event organizers decided to reroute the marathon course to showcase more scenic areas of the city.

senses.definition: to turn something aside or cause it to deviate from its original path or direction

examples.x: The air traffic controller instructed the pilot to swerve the plane to the right to avoid potential turbulence.
The co-driver effectively communicated to the driver when to swerve the rally car.
The driver swerved the vehicle to avoid a large pothole on the road.
The operator skillfully swerved the remote-controlled car around obstacles.
The ski instructor taught beginners how to swerve their skis to control speed.

senses.definition: (particularly of a horn or electronic device) to make a short, often high-pitched sound as a signal or alert

examples.x: The car behind us beeped impatiently as we waited at the green light.
When the microwave finishes heating, it will beep to signal that the food is ready.
The alarm clock beeped loudly, waking me up from a deep sleep.
The cashier scanned the groceries, and the checkout machine beeped with each item.
The hospital equipment beeped regularly, indicating the patient's vital signs.

senses.definition: to cause a horn, particularly of a vehicle, to make a loud noise

examples.x: The taxi driver honked the horn to alert pedestrians of his presence.
The excited children honked the bicycle horn as they rode through the neighborhood.
The referee honked the air horn to signal the end of the game.
The ship's captain honked the horn to alert nearby vessels in the fog.
She honks to greet her friend waiting on the sidewalk.

senses.definition: to adjust the angle or intensity of the headlights, typically to prevent dazzling or blinding other drivers on the road

examples.x: While driving oncoming traffic, drivers are expected to dip their headlights to avoid glare.
The automatic headlight system detected the presence of oncoming vehicles and automatically dipped the lights.
She forgot to dip her headlights, causing discomfort to the driver in front of her.
Drivers are reminded to dip their lights in foggy conditions to improve visibility for all road users.

senses.definition: (of a vehicle) to behave or respond in a particular way when being driven or controlled

examples.x: The car handles smoothly on rough roads.
How does the truck handle on rough terrain?
She was impressed by how the sedan handles on winding roads.

senses.definition: to move in front of another vehicle that is going more slowly

examples.x: She passes the truck on the highway every morning.
He passed the slow car carefully last night.
The bus is passing a bicycle right now.
They will pass the tractor on the narrow road tomorrow.
The driver had passed many vehicles before reaching the city.

senses.definition: to drive a vehicle into the space in front of another, providing minimal room for the latter to navigate comfortably

examples.x: Frustrated by the slow-moving traffic, he decided to cut in, narrowly squeezing into the lane.
The aggressive driver abruptly cut in, causing the car behind to slam on its brakes to avoid a collision.
During rush hour, some motorists impatiently cut in, creating tension and potential hazards on the road.
The defensive driver maintained a safe following distance, avoiding the need to cut in abruptly.

senses.definition: to give way or surrender to another vehicle or person, typically while driving

examples.x: Drivers must yield to pedestrians at crosswalks to ensure their safety.
The car yielded to the ambulance, allowing it to pass quickly through the heavy traffic.
The cyclist yielded to the car approaching from behind and moved to the side of the road.
Please yield to merging traffic when entering the highway to prevent accidents.

senses.definition: to run an engine slowly without being engaged in any work or gear

examples.x: The car idled at the traffic light, waiting for the signal to change.
The generator idled quietly during the power outage.
The boat idled in the harbor while the crew prepared for departure.
The motorcycle idled smoothly while the rider checked the map.
The airplane idled on the runway, awaiting clearance for takeoff.

senses.definition: to not move anymore

examples.x: The car stopped at the pedestrian crosswalk.
The train doesn't stop on this route after 6 PM.
The traffic light turned red, so we had to stop at the intersection.

senses.definition: to signal or direct a driver to move their vehicle to the side of the road
to make a vehicle stop its movement

examples.x: As the traffic light turned red, he had to pull up the car abruptly.
The valet was quick to pull up the car when the guests were ready to leave.

senses.definition: to move a vehicle to the side or off the main road, often into a designated area
to direct a vehicle to move to the side of the road or to another location where it can stop

examples.x: As I approached the school, I pulled in to drop off my daughter.
Seeing a rest area ahead, he pulled in to take a break.
The taxi pulled in outside the hotel to pick up passengers.
As the rain started pouring, I decided to pull in and wait it out.
Driving for hours, he was relieved to see a rest stop and pulled in.

senses.definition: to move a car, bus, etc. into an empty place and leave it there for a short time

examples.x: After circling the block for several minutes, she finally found a spot to park her car.
The bus driver skillfully parked the bus at the designated stop.
The commuters hurriedly parked their bicycles in the designated area before catching the train.
The valet attendant quickly parked the guests' cars in an organized row.
As the family reached the amusement park, they began looking for a suitable place to park their minivan.

senses.definition: to park a vehicle alongside another parked vehicle
to block the path of another vehicle by parking too closely

examples.x: Cars were frequently blocked in due to the limited parking space.
A line of parked cars was blocking in those attempting to leave.

senses.definition: the act of shifting from one gear to another in a vehicle

examples.x: When driving a manual car, smooth gear changes are important for maintaining control and fuel efficiency.
The instructor taught me how to perform a gear change smoothly during my driving lessons.
A sudden gear change can cause the car to jerk, so it's best to accelerate smoothly.
The gearbox in this car allows for quick gear changes, which is great for city driving.
He noticed a problem with the gear change mechanism and took the car to the mechanic for repairs.

senses.definition: to choose the direction of and guide a vehicle, ship, etc., especially by using a map

examples.x: She helped the driver navigate by providing turn-by-turn directions.
The travel agent provided a detailed itinerary, helping the vacationers navigate through scenic routes.
The driving instructor taught the student how to navigate through traffic and use road signs.
Using a paper map, the backpackers took turns navigating.
The navigator instructed the driver on how to navigate through diverse landscapes and terrains.

senses.definition: a detailed plan or guide of roads and routes

examples.x: He consulted the road map to find the quickest route.
She marked important landmarks on the road map for reference.
They used a digital road map app for navigation.
The road map showed alternate routes in case of traffic.
It's always good to have a road map handy for long trips.

senses.definition: the act of starting a vehicle with a discharged battery using power from another vehicle's battery
to start a vehicle by pushing it while in gear, typically when the engine fails to start normally
to measure or record the speed of something

examples.x: The car clocked 100 kilometers per hour during the race.
They are clocking the speed of the new train to ensure it meets safety standards.
Yesterday, the cyclist clocked a record speed on the downhill track.

senses.definition: (of a mechanical device) to function or move smoothly and quietly

examples.x: As the car cruised along the highway, the engine purred softly, a testament to its well-maintained condition.
After the mechanic repaired the faulty components, the motorcycle engine began to purr smoothly.
With regular maintenance, the air conditioning unit purred quietly.
The sewing machine hummed and purred as it stitched fabric together
The espresso machine purred as it brewed the perfect cup of coffee.

senses.definition: to run an engine of a vehicle very quickly

examples.x: He gunned the engine of his motorcycle, reveling in the roar and the rush of acceleration.
As soon as the traffic light turned green, she gunned the car, eager to get ahead of the pack.
The racer gunned his vehicle down the straightaway, pushing it to its maximum speed.
Impatient with the slow-moving traffic, he gunned the engine to signal his frustration.
The thrill-seeker gunned the ATV up the steep hill, enjoying the adrenaline rush of the climb.
